notes: IMPORTANT: contrary to the folder's ABSTRACT-ONLY.md marker (which the task described as indicating this paper is abstract-only/paywalled), the downloaded main PDF in this repo actually contains the FULL 6-page article (Introduction, Tables 1-4, Discussion, References) -- extraction was performed from the complete text, not just the abstract. This paper's own primary focus is on penetrance of DEAFNESS as a WS1 clinical feature (modifier-gene hypothesis), not on the molecular mechanism of the pigmentary phenotype per se; the PAX3-transcription-factor/neural-crest-expression mechanism for the syndrome (including its pigmentary component) is stated as established background, not this paper's novel finding.

classification_notes: RAB32 not canonical, but Discussion explicitly states a demonstrated mechanism: 'KITLG, RAB32, and SPIRE2 in melanosome transport or dispersion within melanocytes' (citing refs 35,36) -- and RAB32 is in the 12-of-36 highest-melanocyte-expression set. mygene.info independently confirms: 'appears to be involved in autophagy and melanosome secretion' -- strong convergent non-canonical candidate with an explicit, specific melanosome-trafficking mechanism from TWO independent sources (paper's Discussion + mygene.info). population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: 16q24.3 locus. Nearest-gene label is SPIRE2 (not canonical); all three genes (SPIRE2, DEF8, CPNE7) asterisked = colocalized eQTL in sun-exposed lower leg skin. CRITICAL near-gene case: this locus is ~70kb from the separate MC1R lead variant (idx24) at the same cytogenetic band, but the paper explicitly distinguishes the two signals and states 'the eQTLs of the colocalized genes...were in LD with the lead variant in SPIRE2' -- i.e., the regulatory evidence points to SPIRE2/DEF8/CPNE7 specifically, NOT to MC1R, despite MC1R's well-known function and proximity. Classified as effector_uncertain (demonstrated regulatory evidence for non-canonical genes), NOT regulatory_of_canonical_neighbour, because the authors explicitly reject the MC1R attribution for this particular variant. SPIRE2 also appears in the melanosome-transport mechanism sentence ('KITLG, RAB32, and SPIRE2 in melanosome transport or dispersion') giving it independent corroborating evidence beyond eQTL colocalization.
